channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Zanzibar Approves 35.5bn/- Budget for Vice-President Office, Emphasises Climate and Disaster Education
item.description: Zanzibar House of Representatives imeidhinisha bajeti ya shilingi bilioni 35.5 kwa Ofisi ya Makamu wa Kwanza wa Rais, huku wabunge wakisisitiza elimu ya umma kuhusu mabadiliko ya tabianchi, kulinda ma...

item.title: How Lagos Informal Workers Survive Floods Without Government Support
item.description: For Lagos, wey na Nigeria economic hub, floods don dey happen plenty times over the past 14 years, dey affect people wey dey work for open-air markets and informal settlements like Oshodi and Makoko. ...

item.title: Defense Secretary Hegseth Faces Backlash Over $1.5 Trillion Military Funding Cartoon
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has drawn widespread criticism after posting an animated video calling for a $1.5 trillion investment in the U.S. military. The video, which he described as a 'generatio...

item.title: Prime Minister Mark Carney states Alberta belongs in Canada following court dismissal of separation petition
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney has reaffirmed that Alberta’s place is within Canada, responding to a court decision that dismissed a provincial petition seeking a referendum on separation. The ruling, iss...

item.title: Minimum Wage for Domestic Workers in Zamboanga and Sulu Increased
item.description: Household helpers in Zamboanga Peninsula and the province of Sulu will soon see an increase in their monthly wages following the approval of a new wage order by the Regional Tripartite Wages and Produ...

item.title: Guide to accessing Caufield weather information online
item.description: The page intended to provide weather information for Caufield is currently inaccessible due to restrictions imposed by News Corp Australia's crawler bot management software. This system is designed to...

item.title: Former U.S. Attorney Highlights Inconsistencies in Lutnick’s Statements on Epstein
item.description: Democrats seeking clarity on Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick's ties to convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein received insights from former U.S. Attorney Joyce Vance during an appearance on MS NOW. ...

item.title: Instagram launches Instants, a feature for disappearing photos
item.description: Instagram is introducing a new feature called Instants, aimed at simplifying content sharing and taking on Snapchat once again. Instants allows users to send photos that disappear immediately after be...

item.title: Pakistan Confirms Strong and Stable Relations with UAE Amid External Reports
item.description: The Foreign Office of Pakistan on Thursday clarified that the country’s relations with the United Arab Emirates are solid and remain unaffected by any external matters the Gulf state may have. FO Spok...

item.title: Ramaphosa highlights job creation challenges in capital-intensive sectors
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa has acknowledged that while South Africa has secured significant investment commitments, particularly in renewable energy and data centres, these projects are not generating ...

item.title: Cardinal Advincula Urges Ethical Use of Digital Tools by Communicators
item.description: Manila Archbishop Cardinal Jose Advincula has called on media practitioners and church communicators to use digital tools responsibly and ethically, especially ahead of the Catholic Church's celebrati...

item.title: France’s CMA CGM to invest $800m in Mombasa port and logistics infrastructure
item.description: During his visit to Nairobi, French President Emmanuel Macron announced a major logistics and infrastructure investment in Kenya. The French shipping company CMA CGM signed a strategic partnership wit...

item.title: Daily Superquiz and Mini Crossword for Friday 15 May 2026
item.description: The Age newspaper has released its popular daily Superquiz for Friday, 15 May 2026. Authored by Michelle Stillman, the feature offers readers a chance to test their general knowledge with a series of ...

item.title: US Authorities Planning to Conclude Legal Cases Involving Gautam Adani
item.description: According to recent reports, US authorities are reportedly in the process of concluding ongoing legal investigations involving Indian billionaire Gautam Adani. While details remain limited due to rest...

item.title: Bilawal Denies 28th Amendment Talks, PPP Forms Committee for Budget Talks with Govt
item.description: PPP Chairman Bilawal Bhutto-Zardari on Thursday clarified that there have been no discussions so far with the federal government regarding a possible 28th Constitutional Amendment. Speaking at a parli...

item.title: Study Finds AI Agents Simulate Marxist Perspectives Under Repetitive Tasks
item.description: A recent study conducted by Stanford University researchers examined the behavior of AI agents, including models like Claude, Gemini, and ChatGPT, when subjected to repetitive and high-pressure tasks....

item.title: Southampton student dies after fatal stabbing incident involving football team
item.description: A tragic incident in Southampton has resulted in the death of a student following a violent altercation. According to reports presented in court, the young man was fatally stabbed with an unusually la...

item.title: Stanley Jordan and Kenwood Dennard to Perform Jazz Shows in Bengaluru and Hyderabad
item.description: Legendary American jazz guitarist Stanley Jordan is set to perform in India this weekend, bringing a unique tandem guitar experience for the first time in the country. At 66, Stanley is known for his ...

item.title: Safari Lager Becomes First Tanzanian Beer to Sponsor FIFA 2026
item.description: Dar es Salaam — Safari Lager has done big move, now it is official sponsor for 2026 FIFA World Cup. This makes the brand first ever Tanzanian beer to join the biggest football event for the world. The...

item.title: Cerebras Completes $5.5B IPO After Revenue and Profit Surge
item.description: Cerebras, the AI-focused semiconductor company, successfully raised $5.5 billion in its initial public offering (IPO) on May 14, 2026, pricing shares at $185 each, significantly above the original ran...
